dverse 0.0.1.9000
  • Reference
    • Get started
    • Meta package
  • Changelog

dverse

Source: vignettes/articles/start.Rmd
start.Rmd

Basics 

library(dplyr, warn.conflicts = FALSE)
# Use dverse and the universe of packages you want to document
library(dverse)
library(glue)
library(tibble)

universe <- c("glue", "tibble")
url_template <- "https://{package}.tidyverse.org/reference/{topic}.html"
documentation <- document_universe(universe, url_template = url_template)

# Note that {topic} and {pacakge} refer to column names
documentation
#> # A tibble: 39 × 5
#>    topic                                             alias title concept package
#>    <chr>                                             <chr> <chr> <chr>   <chr>  
#>  1 <a href=https://tibble.tidyverse.org/reference/a… add_… Add … additi… tibble 
#>  2 <a href=https://tibble.tidyverse.org/reference/a… add_… Add … additi… tibble 
#>  3 <a href=https://glue.tidyverse.org/reference/as_… as_g… Coer… NA      glue   
#>  4 <a href=https://tibble.tidyverse.org/reference/a… as_t… Coer… NA      tibble 
#>  5 <a href=https://tibble.tidyverse.org/reference/c… char… Form… vector… tibble 
#>  6 <a href=https://tibble.tidyverse.org/reference/d… digi… Comp… NA      tibble 
#>  7 <a href=https://tibble.tidyverse.org/reference/e… enfr… Conv… NA      tibble 
#>  8 <a href=https://glue.tidyverse.org/reference/eng… engi… Cust… NA      glue   
#>  9 <a href=https://tibble.tidyverse.org/reference/e… exte… Exte… NA      tibble 
#> 10 <a href=https://tibble.tidyverse.org/reference/f… form… Colu… NA      tibble 
#> # ℹ 29 more rows

You may generate a clickable table is with knitr::kable().

pick <- select(documentation, topic, title, concept, package)

# Removing column names for cleaner output
knitr::kable(head(pick), col.names = NULL)
add_column Add columns to a data frame addition tibble
add_row Add rows to a data frame addition tibble
as_glue Coerce object to glue NA glue
as_tibble Coerce lists, matrices, and more to data frames NA tibble
char Format a character vector vector classes tibble
digits Comparing display with data frames NA tibble

You may generate a searchable table with DT::datatable().

# `escape = FALSE` shows the link (defaults to showing the underlying URL)
pick |> DT::datatable(escape = FALSE)

Organization and customization

In a meta-package you may organize the documentation of the entire universe in a single place, maybe organized by package or concept (via #' @family). You may create your own custom tables with DT or other packages.

custom_table <- function(data, search = "") {
  DT::datatable(
    data,
    escape = FALSE,
    rownames = NULL,
    options = list(
      # fti =  f: filter, t: table, i: information
      dom = "fti", search = list(search = search)
    )
  )
}
  • By package
custom_table(filter(pick, package == "glue"), search = "safe")
  • By concept
custom_table(filter(pick, concept == first(concept)))

Presentation

In a meta-package, you may use dverse in an article, then use it to override the default Reference component.

For an example see the pkgdown Reference of dverse and follow the link to its source and see how it’s configured in _pkgdown.yml.

On this page

Developed by Mauro Lepore.

Site built with pkgdown 2.1.1.
